title: Add namespace-scoped RBAC and namespace flag
description:
**Size:** 📦 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Source:** GitHub Issue [#123](https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/123)

1. Add `--namespace` string flag to `internal/common/bootstrap/manager.go` alongside existing flags (lines 66-84); when set, configure `cache.Options{DefaultNamespaces: map[string]cache.Config{ns: {}}}` at line 91.
2. Add `rbac.namespaceScoped: false` toggle to `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.yaml`.
3. Create `templates/role.yaml` (new) mirroring the 12 apiGroup rule blocks from `clusterrole.yaml`, guarded by `{{- if .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped }}`.
4. Create `templates/rolebinding.yaml` (new) binding ServiceAccount to Role, same conditional.
5. Wrap existing `templates/clusterrole.yaml` and `templates/clusterrolebinding.yaml` in `{{- if not .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped }}`.
6. Update `templates/deployment.yaml` to conditionally add `--namespace={{ .Release.Namespace }}` container arg when `rbac.namespaceScoped: true` (follow existing `--leader-elect` conditional pattern).
7. Add multi-tenant deployment documentation describing when to use namespace-scoped mode, webhook limitation caveat (recommend `webhook.enabled: false`), and CRD installation remains cluster-admin concern.

Verification: `helm template` with defaults produces ClusterRole (no Role); with `rbac.namespaceScoped=true` produces Role (no ClusterRole) and `--namespace` flag. `helm lint` passes both configs. Bootstrap tests verify flag populates cache options.

**Rationale:** ClusterRole grants operator permissions across all namespaces, violating least-privilege in multi-tenant deployments. A compromised operator with ClusterRole can access resources in every namespace. Namespace-scoped RBAC limits blast radius to a single namespace, enabling secure multi-tenant patterns where each tenant gets an isolated operator instance.

**Affected Areas:**
- internal/common/bootstrap/manager.go
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/clusterrole.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/clusterrolebinding.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/role.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/rolebinding.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/deployment.yaml
- docs/

stories.title: Cluster admin deploys operator with default cluster-wide RBAC
stories.want: the operator to deploy with ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding by default
stories.so_that: existing deployments continue working without configuration changes and the operator can manage resources across all namespaces
criteria: helm template with default values produces ClusterRole with 14 rule blocks
helm template with default values produces ClusterRoleBinding binding ServiceAccount to ClusterRole
helm template with default values does NOT produce Role or RoleBinding
Deployment container args do NOT include --namespace flag with default values
helm lint passes with default values

stories.title: Platform engineer deploys operator with namespace-scoped RBAC
stories.want: to deploy the operator scoped to a single namespace using rbac.namespaceScoped=true
stories.so_that: the operator only has permissions within its namespace, enabling secure multi-tenant isolation
criteria: helm template with rbac.namespaceScoped=true produces Role with 14 rule blocks identical to ClusterRole
helm template with rbac.namespaceScoped=true produces RoleBinding binding ServiceAccount to Role
helm template with rbac.namespaceScoped=true does NOT produce ClusterRole or ClusterRoleBinding
Deployment container args include --namespace={{ .Release.Namespace }} when rbac.namespaceScoped=true
helm lint passes with rbac.namespaceScoped=true

stories.title: Developer uses --namespace flag in bootstrap to restrict cache
stories.want: the shared bootstrap package to accept a --namespace flag that restricts the controller-runtime cache to a single namespace
stories.so_that: both keystone and c5c3 operators can run in namespace-scoped mode without code changes to their main.go
criteria: --namespace string flag is registered alongside existing flags in bootstrap.Run()
When --namespace is set, cache.Options.DefaultNamespaces is populated with that namespace
When --namespace is not set, cache.Options has no DefaultNamespaces (watches all namespaces)
SyncPeriod remains configured regardless of --namespace value

stories.title: Platform operator follows multi-tenant deployment documentation
stories.want: documentation explaining when and how to use namespace-scoped mode with known caveats
stories.so_that: I can correctly configure the operator for multi-tenant environments without hitting known pitfalls
criteria: Documentation explains when to use namespace-scoped mode vs cluster-wide mode
Documentation includes webhook limitation caveat with recommendation to set webhook.enabled=false
Documentation notes that CRD installation remains a cluster-admin concern
Documentation provides example Helm values for namespace-scoped deployment

stories.title: CI validates both RBAC modes produce valid manifests
stories.want: the CI pipeline to validate helm template rendering for both cluster-wide and namespace-scoped configurations
stories.so_that: regressions in either RBAC mode are caught before merge
criteria: CI helm-validate job includes a scenario with rbac.namespaceScoped=true
helm template succeeds for namespace-scoped configuration
helm unittest passes for all new and updated test files

requirements.description: The bootstrap package SHALL accept a --namespace string flag alongside existing flags (--metrics-bind-address, --health-probe-bind-address, --leader-elect, --sync-period)
requirements.rationale: The --namespace flag is the runtime mechanism that tells the operator to restrict its cache and reconciliation to a single namespace, enabling namespace-scoped operation for both keystone and c5c3 operators via the shared bootstrap package

scenarios.name: Namespace flag parsed with value
scenarios.when: the operator binary is started with --namespace=tenant-a
scenarios.then: the namespace variable is populated with 'tenant-a'

scenarios.name: Namespace flag defaults to empty
scenarios.when: the operator binary is started without --namespace
scenarios.then: the namespace variable is empty string

scenarios.name: Namespace flag coexists with existing flags
scenarios.when: the operator binary is started with --namespace=tenant-a --leader-elect --sync-period=5m
scenarios.then: all flags are parsed correctly without conflict

requirements.description: When --namespace is set, the controller-runtime cache SHALL be restricted to that single namespace via cache.Options.DefaultNamespaces
requirements.rationale: Restricting the cache ensures the operator only watches and reconciles resources in its designated namespace, preventing cross-tenant data access even if RBAC rules were misconfigured

scenarios.name: Cache restricted to single namespace
scenarios.then: cache.Options.DefaultNamespaces contains exactly one entry: {'tenant-a': {}}
and_then: SyncPeriod is still set to the configured value

scenarios.name: Cache unrestricted when namespace empty
scenarios.then: cache.Options.DefaultNamespaces is nil
and_then: operator watches resources across all namespaces

scenarios.name: SyncPeriod preserved with namespace
scenarios.then: cache.Options has both DefaultNamespaces and SyncPeriod configured

requirements.description: The Helm chart SHALL provide an rbac.namespaceScoped toggle in values.yaml, defaulting to false
requirements.rationale: A Helm values toggle allows operators to switch between cluster-wide and namespace-scoped RBAC without modifying templates, following the established pattern (e.g., webhook.enabled, leaderElection.enabled)

scenarios.name: Default value is false
scenarios.then: rbac.namespaceScoped is false

scenarios.name: Override to true via --set
scenarios.when: helm install is run with --set rbac.namespaceScoped=true
scenarios.then: namespace-scoped RBAC templates are rendered
and_then: cluster-wide RBAC templates are not rendered

scenarios.name: Value accessible in all templates
scenarios.then: the value is accessible in clusterrole.yaml, clusterrolebinding.yaml, role.yaml, rolebinding.yaml, and deployment.yaml templates

requirements.description: When rbac.namespaceScoped is true, the chart SHALL render a namespace-scoped Role with identical rules to the existing ClusterRole (all 14 rule blocks)
requirements.rationale: The namespace-scoped Role must grant the exact same permissions as the ClusterRole but limited to the release namespace, ensuring the operator can function identically within its namespace scope

scenarios.name: Role rendered with correct kind and metadata
scenarios.then: a Role resource is rendered with kind: Role, correct fullname, and standard labels
and_then: the Role is namespace-scoped (no cluster scope)

scenarios.name: Role contains all 14 rule blocks
scenarios.then: the Role's rules array contains exactly 14 entries matching the ClusterRole rules
and_then: keystones CRUD, keystones/status, keystones/finalizers, apps/deployments, core resources, events, batch, mariadb, external-secrets, rbac, policy, autoscaling, networking, coordination

scenarios.name: Role not rendered when namespaceScoped is false
scenarios.then: no Role resource is rendered

requirements.description: When rbac.namespaceScoped is true, the chart SHALL render a RoleBinding binding the ServiceAccount to the namespace-scoped Role
requirements.rationale: The RoleBinding connects the operator's ServiceAccount to the Role, granting namespace-scoped permissions analogous to how the ClusterRoleBinding connects to the ClusterRole

scenarios.name: RoleBinding references correct Role
scenarios.then: a RoleBinding is rendered with roleRef.kind=Role and roleRef.name matching the fullname

scenarios.name: RoleBinding binds correct ServiceAccount
scenarios.then: the RoleBinding's subjects list contains the operator ServiceAccount

scenarios.name: RoleBinding not rendered when namespaceScoped is false
scenarios.then: no RoleBinding resource is rendered

requirements.description: When rbac.namespaceScoped is false (default), the chart SHALL render ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding; when true, they SHALL NOT be rendered
requirements.rationale: Mutual exclusion between cluster-wide and namespace-scoped RBAC prevents conflicting permission grants and ensures a clean, predictable RBAC configuration

scenarios.name: ClusterRole rendered by default
scenarios.then: ClusterRole is rendered with all 14 rule blocks
and_then: existing tests continue to pass without modification

scenarios.name: ClusterRole not rendered when namespace-scoped
scenarios.then: ClusterRole template produces no documents

scenarios.name: ClusterRoleBinding not rendered when namespace-scoped
scenarios.then: ClusterRoleBinding template produces no documents

requirements.description: When rbac.namespaceScoped is true, the Deployment SHALL include --namespace={{ .Release.Namespace }} as a container arg
requirements.rationale: The --namespace container arg tells the bootstrap manager to restrict its cache to the release namespace, completing the namespace-scoped isolation chain from Helm values through RBAC to runtime cache configuration

scenarios.name: Namespace arg present when namespace-scoped
scenarios.then: the container args include --namespace={{ .Release.Namespace }}
and_then: the arg appears alongside existing args (--leader-elect, --metrics-bind-address, --health-probe-bind-address)

scenarios.name: Namespace arg absent when cluster-wide
scenarios.then: the container args do NOT include --namespace

scenarios.name: Follows existing conditional arg pattern
scenarios.when: rbac.namespaceScoped is true and leaderElection.enabled is true
scenarios.then: both --leader-elect and --namespace args are present

requirements.description: The project SHALL include documentation describing the multi-tenant deployment pattern with namespace-scoped RBAC, including the webhook limitation caveat
requirements.rationale: Namespace-scoped mode has important caveats (webhook incompatibility, CRD cluster-admin requirement) that operators must understand before adopting multi-tenant deployments

scenarios.name: Documentation covers when to use namespace-scoped mode
scenarios.when: a platform operator reads the multi-tenant deployment documentation
scenarios.then: they find a clear explanation of when cluster-wide vs namespace-scoped mode is appropriate
and_then: security rationale for namespace scoping is explained

scenarios.name: Documentation includes webhook caveat
scenarios.when: a platform operator reads the multi-tenant deployment documentation
scenarios.then: they find a prominent caveat that webhooks are cluster-scoped and should be disabled in namespace-scoped mode
and_then: recommendation to set webhook.enabled=false is explicit

scenarios.name: Documentation notes CRD installation requirement
scenarios.when: a platform operator reads the multi-tenant deployment documentation
scenarios.then: they find that CRD installation remains a cluster-admin concern regardless of RBAC mode

tasks.title: Add --namespace flag to bootstrap manager.go with cache restriction logic and unit tests (REQ-001, REQ-002)
tasks.description: In internal/common/bootstrap/manager.go: (1) Add a `var namespace string` variable and `flag.StringVar(&namespace, "namespace", "", ...)` alongside existing flags at lines 66-80. (2) Extract a `cacheOptions(syncPeriod time.Duration, namespace string) cache.Options` helper function that builds cache.Options with SyncPeriod and conditionally sets DefaultNamespaces when namespace is non-empty. (3) Use the helper in Run() at line 91. In internal/common/bootstrap/manager_test.go: Add TestCacheOptions_withNamespace, TestCacheOptions_withoutNamespace, TestCacheOptions_syncPeriodPreserved, TestCacheOptions_singleNamespaceEntry. Follow existing test style (standard library testing, Feature: CC-0043 comment).

tasks.title: Add rbac.namespaceScoped to values.yaml and wrap clusterrole/clusterrolebinding in conditional guards (REQ-003, REQ-006)
tasks.description: In values.yaml: Add `rbac:\n  namespaceScoped: false` section. In templates/clusterrole.yaml: Add `{{- if not .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped }}` at line 1 and `{{- end }}` at the end. In templates/clusterrolebinding.yaml: Same conditional wrapping. In tests/clusterrole_test.yaml: Add test 'should not render when rbac.namespaceScoped is true' with `set: rbac.namespaceScoped: true` and `asserts: - hasDocuments: count: 0`. In tests/clusterrolebinding_test.yaml: Same pattern. Verify all existing tests still pass with default values (namespaceScoped=false).

tasks.title: Create templates/role.yaml with all 14 rule blocks and tests/role_test.yaml (REQ-004)
tasks.description: Create templates/role.yaml: Copy clusterrole.yaml content, change kind from ClusterRole to Role, wrap in `{{- if .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped }}`. All 14 rule blocks must be identical to clusterrole.yaml. Create tests/role_test.yaml: Add suite with tests: (1) 'should not render when rbac.namespaceScoped is false (default)' with `asserts: - hasDocuments: count: 0`, (2) 'should render when rbac.namespaceScoped is true' with `set: rbac.namespaceScoped: true` verifying kind=Role and rule count=14, (3) individual rule verification tests for keystones CRUD, status, finalizers, deployments, core resources, events, batch, mariadb, external-secrets, rbac, policy, autoscaling, networking, leases. Follow clusterrole_test.yaml structure.

tasks.title: Create templates/rolebinding.yaml and tests/rolebinding_test.yaml (REQ-005)
tasks.description: Create templates/rolebinding.yaml: Mirror clusterrolebinding.yaml structure but use kind: RoleBinding and roleRef.kind: Role. Wrap in `{{- if .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped }}`. Use same template helpers (fullname, labels, serviceAccountName). Create tests/rolebinding_test.yaml: Add suite with tests: (1) 'should not render when rbac.namespaceScoped is false' with `asserts: - hasDocuments: count: 0`, (2) 'should reference the correct Role' with `set: rbac.namespaceScoped: true` verifying roleRef.kind=Role, roleRef.name, roleRef.apiGroup, (3) 'should bind to the correct ServiceAccount' verifying subjects[0]. Follow clusterrolebinding_test.yaml structure.

tasks.title: Update deployment.yaml with conditional --namespace arg and update deployment_test.yaml (REQ-007)
tasks.description: In templates/deployment.yaml: After the `{{- if .Values.leaderElection.enabled }}` / `{{- end }}` block (lines 31-33), add `{{- if .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped }}\n            - --namespace={{ .Release.Namespace }}\n            {{- end }}`. In tests/deployment_test.yaml: (1) Add test 'should include --namespace arg when rbac.namespaceScoped is true' with `set: rbac.namespaceScoped: true` verifying args contain --namespace=NAMESPACE. (2) Add test 'should include both --leader-elect and --namespace' with both enabled, verifying full args list. (3) Update existing args test description if needed to clarify it covers default values.

tasks.title: Add namespace-scoped helm template scenario to CI helm-validate job (REQ-003, REQ-006)
tasks.description: In .github/workflows/ci.yaml: Add 'Scenario 5: namespace-scoped RBAC' to the 'Helm template' step (after line 313): `echo '--- Scenario 5: namespace-scoped RBAC ---'` followed by `helm template test "$CHART" --set rbac.namespaceScoped=true --set webhook.enabled=false`. This verifies that the namespace-scoped configuration produces valid manifests. The webhook.enabled=false follows the documented recommendation for namespace-scoped deployments.

tasks.title: Add multi-tenant deployment documentation with webhook caveat (REQ-008)
tasks.description: Create docs/how-to/multi-tenant-deployment.md: VitePress frontmatter with title. Sections: (1) Overview - when to use namespace-scoped vs cluster-wide mode, security rationale. (2) Prerequisites - CRDs installed by cluster-admin, namespace exists. (3) Configuration - example Helm values with rbac.namespaceScoped=true and webhook.enabled=false. (4) Webhook Limitation - prominent caveat that MutatingWebhookConfiguration/ValidatingWebhookConfiguration are cluster-scoped resources and must be disabled in namespace-scoped mode. (5) CRD Installation - note that CRDs are always cluster-scoped. Update docs/.vitepress/config.ts sidebar: Add 'How-to Guides' section with 'Multi-tenant Deployment' link.

review_criteria: All helm unittest tests pass: `helm unittest operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/` exits 0 with all tests passing including new role, rolebinding, and updated clusterrole/clusterrolebinding/deployment tests
helm lint passes for both configurations: `helm lint operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/` (defaults) and with `--set rbac.namespaceScoped=true`

helm template with default values produces ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding but NOT Role or RoleBinding; with rbac.namespaceScoped=true produces Role and RoleBinding but NOT ClusterRole or ClusterRoleBinding
Go unit tests pass: `go test ./internal/common/bootstrap/...` exits 0 with new cacheOptions tests passing
go vet passes: `go vet ./internal/common/bootstrap/...` exits 0 with no warnings
Role rules in role.yaml are identical to ClusterRole rules in clusterrole.yaml (14 rule blocks with same apiGroups, resources, and verbs)
Deployment args include --namespace={{ .Release.Namespace }} only when rbac.namespaceScoped=true; default deployment args are unchanged
Documentation covers webhook limitation caveat, CRD installation note, and includes example Helm values for namespace-scoped deployment
implementation_notes:
## Architecture

```
                     values.yaml
                  rbac.namespaceScoped
                         |
          +--------------+--------------+
          | false (default)             | true
          v                             v
  clusterrole.yaml              role.yaml
  clusterrolebinding.yaml       rolebinding.yaml
                         |
                         v
                  deployment.yaml
                  --namespace={{ .Release.Namespace }}
                  (only when true)
                         |
                         v
              bootstrap/manager.go
              --namespace flag
              cache.Options.DefaultNamespaces
```

## Data Flow
1. Helm values `rbac.namespaceScoped` controls which RBAC templates render and whether `--namespace` arg is injected into the Deployment
2. The container arg `--namespace=<ns>` is parsed by `bootstrap.Run()` via Go's `flag` package
3. When the namespace flag is non-empty, `cacheOptions()` sets `cache.Options.DefaultNamespaces` to restrict the controller-runtime informer cache
4. The manager only watches resources in the specified namespace, enforcing namespace isolation at the runtime level in addition to RBAC

## Key Patterns to Follow
- **Conditional rendering**: Follow the `{{- if .Values.webhook.enabled }}` pattern used in deployment.yaml for webhook volumes. Apply same pattern for `rbac.namespaceScoped`.
- **Flag registration**: Follow existing `flag.StringVar` / `flag.BoolVar` / `flag.DurationVar` pattern at lines 66-80 of manager.go.
- **Helm test structure**: Follow existing test pattern in `tests/clusterrole_test.yaml` with suite name, template reference, and assertion style.
- **Feature ID comments**: All new Go code and Helm templates should include `Feature: CC-0043` or `CC-0043` comments.

## Pitfalls
- **Rule sync**: role.yaml rules MUST exactly mirror clusterrole.yaml. Any future RBAC changes must update both files. The review pattern `sync-rbac-markers-with-helm-clusterrole-templates.md` should be extended post-implementation.
- **Webhook incompatibility**: MutatingWebhookConfiguration and ValidatingWebhookConfiguration are cluster-scoped Kubernetes resources. In namespace-scoped mode, the operator cannot create/manage them with only namespace-level RBAC. Documentation must clearly recommend `webhook.enabled: false`.
- **CRDs remain cluster-scoped**: CRD installation is always a cluster-admin task. The operator does not install CRDs itself, so this is not a functional issue, but documentation must clarify this.
- **Flag global state**: `flag.Parse()` uses global state. The `cacheOptions` helper is extracted as a pure function to enable unit testing without flag side effects.
- **Existing test count**: clusterrole_test.yaml asserts `count: 14` for rules. The new role_test.yaml must assert the same count.
